title: CloudFront Origins, OAC, SSE-KMS, and Private Delivery · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds private CloudFront-to-S3 origin contract, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of private CloudFront-to-S3 origin contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
Configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
Scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop S3 Origin Website Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 4581 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop S3 Origin Website Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish regular s3 origins from website endpoints by https, oac, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop S3 Origin Website Boundary, keep S3 Origin Website Boundary connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Oai Oac Migration
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-oai-oac-migration-1om78s" that uses case 4416 to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Oai Oac Migration.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Oai Oac Migration into observable performance evidence. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Oai Oac Migration, keep Oai Oac Migration connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Oai Oac Migration, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Oai Oac Migration precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Oai Oac Migration, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 6914 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Oai Oac Migration, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Replacing the origin identity ID in one policy statement is a zero-risk migration.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Oai Oac Migration
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 8533 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Oai Oac Migration.

stimulus.title: Oai Oac Migration failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Replacing the origin identity ID in one policy statement is a zero-risk migration.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Oai Oac Migration condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Oai Oac Migration happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
